title: IDENTIFICATION ASSISTANCE - 211info
text: TRANSITION PROJECTS IDENTIFICATION ASSISTANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a Determination of Homelessness form needed to apply for an ID Card from the DMV. The agency does not provide drivers licenses or birth certificates. 650 NW Irving Street Portland, OR 97209 Eligibility: Individuals experiencing homelessness Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-7pm, Saturday-Sunday 8:30am-3pm (503) 280-4700 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (503) 280-4700 INTAKE PROCEDURE: Walk in or call for more information. For information about DMV requirements, ask a staff person or call the local DMV. FEES: None; including cost of ID Card from the DMV DOCUMENTS: Backup identification documentation is required. For example, the DMV typically requires a birth certificate before issuing an ID card. LANGUAGES: English, Spanish AREA SERVED: Multnomah County TRAVEL/LOCATION INFORMATION: Two blocks northwest of Union Station. On theTriMet MAX Yellow and Green lines. Services: Records/Licenses/Permits Fee Payment Assistance * Homelessness Issues Other Services Offered At This Location: GENERAL MEDICAL CARE - MEDICAL CLINIC HOUSING/SHELTER - ARGYLE GARDENS HOUSING/SHELTER - BARBARA MAHER APARTMENTS HOUSING/SHELTER - CLARK CENTER HOUSING/SHELTER - DOREEN'S PLACE HOUSING/SHELTER - JEAN'S PLACE HOUSING/SHELTER - RESOURCE CENTER TRANSPORTATION - TRIMET TICKETS If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. TRANSITION PROJECTS
